display.purpose: DayCounter numbers, podium ranks, leaderboard rank column. Bebas Neue only, sizes 2rem to 6rem.

body.purpose: All reading text, task titles, descriptions, form content. Inter regular at 14px.

label.purpose: All UI chrome labels: nav items, stat headers, status badges, section kickers, form labels. JetBrains Mono semibold uppercase at 10px, 0.15em tracking.

shadows.name: button-lift
shadows.purpose: Primary and destructive buttons only. The single persistent shadow in the system. Communicates clickability.

shadows.name: task-complete-ring
shadows.purpose: Transient animated ring on task completion (480ms cubic-bezier ease-out).

shadows.name: journey-today-pulse
shadows.purpose: Pulsing gold ring on current day journey tile (2s infinite).

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (hover, active, expanding). 150–250ms duration range.

motion.name: ease-complete
motion.purpose: Task completion animation (card settle + status ring). 480ms total duration.

motion.name: skeleton-shimmer
motion.purpose: Skeleton loading shimmer sweep. Linear gradient across surface-raised through border highlight.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one call-to-action button. Full pill, habit red, lift shadow. Used for all primary actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border-radius: 9999px; border: none; background: #e63329; color: #ffffff; padding: 12px 24px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1; box-shadow: 0 16px 40px rgba(15,23,42,0.18); cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #c42a22; transform: translateY(-0.5px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e63329; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Task Card
components.description: The central interaction surface. Every challenge task renders as one of these. Expandable with number inputs, tier chips, or sub-point toggles.
components.html: 🏋️Complete morning workoutTAP TO COMPLETE
components.css: .ds-task-card { overflow: hidden; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #2a2a2a; background: #111111; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; } .ds-task-card-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s; } .ds-task-card-row:hover { background: #1a1a1a; } .ds-task-card-icon { font-size: 24px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-task-card-title { flex: 1; min-width: 0; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: #f0f0f0; } .ds-badge-pending { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid #2a2a2a; background: #1a1a1a; color: #6b7280; padding: 4px 12px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; white-space: nowrap; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Task Status Badge - Completed
components.description: Status badge variant for completed tasks. Success green with 10% background tint.
components.css: .ds-badge-completed { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid rgba(34,197,94,0.3); background: rgba(34,197,94,0.1); color: #22c55e; padding: 4px 12px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Task Status Badge - Overdue
components.description: Status badge variant for overdue/missed tasks. Habit red with 10% background tint.
components.css: .ds-badge-overdue { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid rgba(230,51,41,0.3); background: rgba(230,51,41,0.1); color: #e63329; padding: 4px 12px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Nav Link - Active
components.description: Active sidebar navigation item. Habit red 10% background tint with red text.
components.css: .ds-nav-active { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 8px; background: rgba(230,51,41,0.1); color: #e63329; text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.2s; } .ds-nav-icon { font-size: 14px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-nav-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Streak Badge
components.description: Full-pill badge displaying current streak count. Gold text on raised surface. Inline with task cards and profile.
components.css: .ds-streak-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid #2a2a2a; background: #1a1a1a; color: #f5c842; padding: 4px 12px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.15em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Stat Card
components.description: Centered statistic card. Mono label above, Bebas Neue display number below. Used in StatsRow 5-column grid.
components.css: .ds-stat-card { border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #2a2a2a; background: #111111; padding: 16px; text-align: center; } .ds-stat-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6b7280; margin: 0 0 4px 0; } .ds-stat-value { font-family: 'Bebas Neue', sans-serif; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1; color: #f0f0f0; margin: 0; }

components.name: Proof Uploader
components.description: Dashed border upload button for photo proof submissions. Includes camera capture and gallery fallback modes.
components.css: .ds-proof-upload { display: block; width: 100%; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px dashed #2a2a2a; background: #1a1a1a; color: #6b7280; padding: 12px 16px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; text-align: center; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-proof-upload:hover { border-color: #e63329; color: #f0f0f0; }

components.name: Skeleton Shimmer
components.description: Loading placeholder with shimmer animation. Used as the base pattern for all component skeletons.
components.css: .ds-skeleton { border-radius: 8px; background-image: linear-gradient(90deg, #1a1a1a 0%, #2a2a2a 50%, #1a1a1a 100%); background-size: 200% 100%; animation: ds-shimmer 1.5s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-shimmer { 0% { background-position: -200% 0; } 100% { background-position: 200% 0; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-skeleton { animation: none; } }

narrative.overview:
HOBBIT's interface is a gym for discipline — not a spa. It's dark, high-contrast, and urgent. Primary red cuts through the near-black background like a coach shouting your name. Gold marks achievement with earned weight. Silver and bronze reward effort but communicate 'not done yet.' Every surface, every border, every label exists to serve one job: get the user in, prove they did the work, see where they stand, and get out.

The system deliberately rejects enterprise dashboard sprawl. There are no nested data tables, no 50-widget views, no 'export to CSV' energy. Pages are single-purpose and fast. Navigation is a fixed sidebar on desktop and a bottom tab bar on mobile — both using compact mono labels and emoji icons. The layout collapses to a single scrollable column with no side-panels. Density is reserved for the leaderboard table and history view; everywhere else, spacing breathes.

Dark mode is the default and the true identity. A light theme exists as a secondary mode, mapped token-for-token, but the dark palette is where the aggression and energy live. Light mode renders the same layout with softer contrast — it's functional, not the showpiece.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black background with high-contrast text and a single red accent
Bebas Neue display for big numbers, Inter for body, JetBrains Mono for all labels and data
Full-pill buttons and badges; 8–12px card corners
No decorative shadows on cards; shadows exist only on primary buttons
Flat-by-default surfaces, tonal layering for depth (base → surface → raised)
Motion is fast (150–200 ms) and state-driven; no orchestrated page-load sequences
Reduced motion respected everywhere with instant-transition fallbacks
Skeleton shimmer loading states, never blank spinners

rules.name: The One Red Rule
rules.body: Habit Red is the only accent color used for interactive elements. Ember Orange, Gold, Silver, and Bronze are reserved for progress and reward signals — never for clickable UI. If a new interactive element needs color, it uses Habit Red or stays neutral.

rules.name: The Surface Ladder Rule
rules.body: Three surfaces only: base → surface → raised. No fourth layer, no nested cards. If depth requires more layering, the layout is too nested.

rules.name: The Gold Is Earned Rule
rules.body: Gold appears only on earned milestones, active streaks, and first-place podium positions. Never as decoration. Never on pending or empty states.

rules.name: The One Label Rule
rules.body: All UI labels — nav items, stat headers, status badges, form labels, table headers — use JetBrains Mono uppercase at 10px with 0.15em tracking. No exceptions. No Inter labels in chrome.

rules.name: The Display Ceiling Rule
rules.body: Display text (DayCounter, podium ranks) uses Bebas Neue at sizes between 2rem and 6rem. Never on buttons, labels, body text, or data tables.

rules.name: The Font Count Rule
rules.body: Three families total: Bebas Neue, Inter, JetBrains Mono. Never introduce a fourth. If a design calls for another voice, use weight or size variation within these three.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Borders define edges; tonal layers define depth. The only persistent shadow in the entire system is the button lift.

rules.name: The No Ghost Card Rule
rules.body: Never pair border: 1px solid with box-shadow blur ≥ 8px on the same element. Cards use borders. Buttons use shadows. Never both as decoration.

dos: Do use Habit Red (#e63329) as the sole interactive accent. One red for all primary actions, active states, and negative status signals.
Do use JetBrains Mono uppercase at 10px with 0.15em tracking for all UI labels. Consistent label vocabulary across navigation, stats, badges, and form elements.
Do use Bebas Neue for display numbers (day counter, podium ranks, leaderboard rank column). No other typeface for large numbers.
Do use the surface ladder (base → surface → raised) for depth. No shadows on cards. Shadows only on primary buttons.
Do ship a skeleton for every data-loading component. Match the live component's exact layout and dimensions.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — animations must degrade to instant transitions, not disappear entirely.
Do keep pages single-column, single-purpose. No side panels, no nested dashboards. Scroll, don't nest.
Do use the gold palette only for earned/reward states. Gold is never decorative or pending.
donts: Don't introduce a second accent color. Habit Red is the only interactive color. Ember Orange exists only as a gradient endpoint on the progress bar.
Don't use enterprise dashboard patterns — no nested data tables, no multi-widget layouts, no export-to-CSV energy.
Don't use corporate wellness aesthetics — no pastel gradients, no zen/meditation vibes, no 'your journey matters' platitudes.
Don't use cartoonish over-gamification — no confetti cannons for trivial actions, no over-animated mascots. Gamification is grounded by proof submissions.
Don't pair border: 1px solid with box-shadow blur ≥ 8px on the same element. Cards use borders. Buttons use shadows. Never both as decoration.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or list items. Use full borders, background tints, or nothing.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). Solid colors only for all text.
Don't use Inter for UI labels or JetBrains Mono for body text. The font roles are fixed.
Don't introduce a fourth typeface. Three families total: Bebas Neue, Inter, JetBrains Mono.
Don't use display fonts (Bebas Neue) in buttons, labels, or data tables. Display is for big numbers only.
